title: Glycerol stock preparation + storage
description: Make and store a long-term bacterial glycerol stock at -80 C, following the Addgene protocol.

payload.body:
## Purpose

Preserve a bacterial strain (and its plasmid) for years by freezing an overnight culture in glycerol at -80 C.

## Materials

| Item | Amount |
| --- | --- |
| Overnight liquid culture (single colony, selective medium) | 500 uL |
| 50% glycerol, sterile (glycerol diluted in dH2O) | 500 uL |
| 2 mL screw-top tube or cryovial | 1 |

## Steps

1. Grow an overnight liquid culture from a single isolated colony in selective medium.
2. Add 500 uL of the culture to 500 uL of 50% glycerol in a 2 mL screw-top tube or cryovial. The final glycerol concentration is about 25% (most labs use 15-25%).
3. Mix gently by shaking the tube 5-6 times.
4. Cap with a screw top. Snap-top tubes can pop open at -80 C and are not recommended.
5. Store at -80 C. The stock is stable for years.

## Expected result

A frozen stock that revives by scraping a sliver of the frozen surface onto a selective plate or into fresh medium.

## Notes and troubleshooting

- Minimize freeze-thaw cycles; each one shortens shelf life.
- When recovering, keep the vial on dry ice while you scrape so it does not thaw completely.

## Safety

BSL-1 E. coli. Use sterile technique and autoclave cultures before disposal.

## Source

Adapted from the Addgene protocol "Creating a Bacterial Glycerol Stock for Long-term Storage of Plasmids".
https://www.addgene.org/protocols/create-glycerol-stock/
